## Monitor the process tasks `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/processes/{processId}/tasks/{taskId}` ### Get the status of a process task In the response: * `currentStep` is the task associated with the specified `taskID`. * `taskState` is the status of the process task. * `progress` is a double that is typically 1.0. * `result` is a JSON object that indicates whether a failure dump file was created, the `objectID`, and whether the task succeeded. * `failureDumpAvailable` indicates whether this process container or an action within it has a failure dump file. **Note:** For a deletion action, `failureDumpAvailable` is always false. * `nestedResults` indicates whether the actions carried out in the process were successful. This contains details in the form of a JSON array, which also contains some debug information when failures occur. [Get the failure dump file](https://anaplanbulkapi20.docs.apiary.io/ListProcessDefinitions#Check-for-process-failures) to view the complete debug information. * `objectID` is the identifier of the process task or a task that it includes. For instance, if `nestedResults` has `failureDumpAvailable` true, you need the `objectID` within that section to obtain the related 'dump' file. * `taskID` is the ID that you specified in the request. Here is a list of each possible `taskState`: | **Status** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | NOT_STARTED | The task is scheduled to run, but has not started yet. | | IN_PROGRESS | The task is currently running. | | COMPLETE | The task has finished running whether successful or not. | | CANCELLING | The task is currently being canceled. Cancellation is not yet complete. | | CANCELLED | The task has been canceled and the changes have been rolled back. | #### Request ``` curl -X GET / https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/processes/{processId}/tasks/{taskId} \ -H 'authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}' \ -H "Content-Type:application/json" ``` #### Headers * `Authorization:AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` * `Content-Type:application/json` #### Parameters | **Parameter** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | `workspaceId` |

* Required
* Type: string
* Description: The workspace ID
* Example: `8a8b8c8d8e8f8g8i`

| | `modelId` |

* Required
* Type: string
* Description: The model ID
* Example: `A1B2C3D4E5F6A1B2C3D4E5F6A1B2C3D4`

| | `processId` |

* Required
* Type: number
* Description: The process ID
* Example: `118000000002`

| | `taskId` |

* Required
* Type: string
* Description: The task ID
* Example: `5B882A63354A4B45A21C16F3644C9C1B`

| #

get.summary: Workspace-scoped form: Retrieve dimension items for a line item
get.description:
Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/models/{modelId}/lineItems/{lineItemId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items`. ## Retrieve dimension items for a line item `/models/{modelId}/lineItems/{lineItemId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items` Use this call to retrieve IDs, codes, and names for dimension items that apply for the specified line item. **Notes**: * To use this call, you must be a [Workspace Administrator](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Workspace_Administration.html). * This call works with any dimension type. * Your requesting model role must allow read access to the module the line item belongs to. Otherwise, this call returns a `404 Not Found` HTTP status. * The items in the response are ordered as listed in the Anaplan model. * The response returns items within a [flat list](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Dimensions/Lists.html) (no hierarchy). * This returns dimension items that apply to a line item. For the view-level call, see [Retrieve selected items in a dimension](https://anaplanbulkapi20.docs.apiary.io/#RetrieveSelectedItemsDimension) and for the model-level call, see [Retrieve all items in a dimension](https://anaplanbulkapi20.docs.apiary.io/#RetrieveItemsDimension). * This call only supports the retrieval of dimension items where the dimension contains a maximum of 1,000,000 items. If the specified dimension contains more than 1,000,000 dimension items, then the call returns a 400 Bad Request HTTP status, instead of a subset of the dimension items. * Providing a non-existent Line Item ID or Dimension ID returns a 404 Not Found HTTP status. * If the request provides an invalid line item ID or dimension ID this call returns a `400 Bad Request` HTTP status. * In the future, use of this call may be rate limited. ### Request ``` curl -X GET 'https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/models/{modelId}/lineItems/{lineItemId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items' \ -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}' \ -H 'Accept: application/json' ``` ### Request headers | **Header** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` |

* Required

| | `Accept: application/json` |

* Optional

* Required
* Type: `String`
* Description: the ID for the model

get.summary: Workspace-scoped form: Retrieve metadata for dimensions on a view
get.description:
Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/models/{modelId}/views/{viewId}`. ## Retrieve metadata for dimensions on a view `/models/{modelId}/views/{viewId}` Use this call to retrieve the name, IDs, and lists of names for the dimensions (columns, pages, rows) on a specified view. **Notes**: * To use this call, you must be a [Workspace Administrator](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Workspace_Administration.html) * If a view has no dimensions on an axis, the call omits the axis field (for example, no `“rows”` field at all instead of `“rows”: []`) * If a view is the default view, its `name` is set to be the same as the module name * Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response * The `viewId` in this request can also be replaced by any valid `lineItemId`. If the Line Item has a changed dimensionality (has a Subsidiary View), the response returns the metadata applicable to that line item. Otherwise, the response returns the default metadata You can use this API call to [map dimensions to cell data export headers](https://help.anaplan.com/43cd0223-edaa-4289-9833-eb9b73666aac-Map-dimensions-to-cell-data-export-headers) or [transform grid CSV format to tabular single column format](https://help.anaplan.com/c8a665b9-6b23-4254-ac68-816657a6e878-Retrieve-the-view-metadata-and-grid-view-data). ### Request ``` curl -X GET 'https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/models/{modelId}/views/{viewId}' \ -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}'\ -H 'Accept: application/json' ``` ### Request headers | **Header** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` |

* Required
* Description: the Anaplan authentication token

| | `Accept: application/json` |

* Required
* Description: This indicates the preferred response is `application/json` format.

| ### Request parameters | **Parameter** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `{modelId}` |

* Required
* Type: String
* Description: the ID for the model
* Example: `A1B2C3D4E5F6A1B2C3D4E5F6A1B2C3`

| | `{viewId}` |

* Required
* Type: Number
* Description: the ID for the view
* Example: `102000000000`

| ### Response 200 Body ``` { "meta": { "schema": "https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/objects/view" }, "status": { "code": 200, "message": "Success" }, "viewName": "REV01 Price Book", "viewId" : "102000000000" "rows": [ { "id": "101000000000", "name": "Products" }, { "id": "101000000003", "name": "Regions" } ], "columns": [ { "id": "101999999999", "name": "Line items" } ], "pages": [ { "id": "101000000001", "name": "Versions" } ], } ```

get.summary: Model-direct form: Retrieve IDs and names for all views in a model
get.description:
Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/views`. ## Retrieve IDs and names for all views in a model `/models/{modelId}/views` Use this call to retrieve the ID and name of all views for a model. The results include default and saved views. If you add the query parameter `includesubsidiaryviews=true`, this includes unsaved subsidiary views in the results. This call also retrieves the ID of the module that the views belong to. **Notes**: * To use this call, you must be a [Workspace Administrator](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Workspace_Administration.html) * Single quotes enclose the module and view names that have special characters * Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response ### Default views For default views: * The value of the {viewId} is identical to the value of the {moduleId} * The value of the name is identical to the value of the module ### Saved views For saved views: * The `name` consists of the module name, a period, and the saved view name. For example, `REP01 Profit & Loss Report.P & L by Country` For unsaved subsidiary views: * The name consists of the module name and the line item name that the subsidiary view is created from * These are separated by a period(.) ### Request ``` curl -X GET 'https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/models/{modelId}/views' \ -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}' \ -H 'Accept: application/json' ``` ### Request headers | **Header** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` |

* Required
* Description: the Anaplan authentication token

| | `Accept: application/json` |

* Required
* Description: This indicates the preferred response is `application/json` format.

| ### Request parameters | **Parameter** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `{modelId}` |

* Required
* Type: String
* Description: the ID for the model
* Example: `A1B2C3D4E5F6A1B2C3D4E5F6A1B2C3`

| ### Query parameter | **Parameter** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `{includesubsidiaryviews}` |

* Optional (default false)
* Type: String
* Description: Whether the response should include unsaved subsidiary views. The field is interpreted as false if anything other than `true` is specified
* Example: `?includesubsidiaryviews=true`

| ### Response header `Content-Type: application/json`

Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items`. ## Retrieve all items in a dimension `/models/{modelGuid}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items` Use this call to retrieve the IDs, codes, and names for items in a specified dimension. The dimension must be a list, a list subset, a line item subset, or the Users dimension. Calls at model-level do not support the Time dimension. The calls return a `400` response. To avoid this, use the view dimension instead. **Notes**: * To use this call, you must be a [Workspace Administrator](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Workspace_Administration.html). * This call is on the model-level. It returns all dimension items as it does not have view-level hiding, filtering, or [Selective Access](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Users/Selective_Access.html). For the view-level endpoint, see [Retrieve selected items in a dimension](https://anaplanbulkapi20.docs.apiary.io/#RetrieveSelectedItemsDimension). * If codes have not been set for an item, then the response excludes the `code` field. * This call only supports the retrieval of dimensions and items from a dimension that contains a maximum of 1,000,000 items. The call returns a `400` response for any more than that number of items. * Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response. ### Request ``` curl -X GET 'https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/models/{modelGuid}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items'\ -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}'\ -H 'Accept: application/json' ``` ### Request headers | **Header** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` |

post.summary: Model-direct form: Lookup dimension items by name or code
post.description:
Model-direct alternate URL form — identical behaviour to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items` POST. ## Lookup dimension items by name or code Retrieve the items from a dimension that match one of a list of names or a list or codes. ** Notes: ** * To use this call, you must be a [Workspace Administrator](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Workspace_Administration.html). * This call does not return results for names or codes for which an item does not exist. * If the given workspace, model, or dimension do not exist, this call returns a `404` code. * If the dimension does not support codes and codes are provided, this call returns a `400` code. * In the future, use of this call may be rate limited. ### Requests that support this feature ``` curl -X POST https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/dimensions/{dimensionId}/items \ -H ‘Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -H 'Accept: application/json' --data-raw '' ``` ### Request headers | **Header** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` |

* Required
* Description: the Anaplan authentication token

| | `Content-Type: application/json` |

* Required
* Description: This indicates the request format is `application/json`.

| | `Accept: application/json` |

* Optional
* Description: This indicates the preferred response is either `application/json` format. Note that this call only outputs in `application/json` format.

| ### Request parameters | **Parameter** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `{workspaceId}` |

* Required in the supported request
* Type: String
* Description: The workspace ID
* Example: `c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6`

| | `{modelId}` |

* Lists

If the request body does not include at least one of these fields, the response returns an error.

get.description:
Alternate URL form — response is equivalent to `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/lists/{listId}`. ## Retrieve list metadata `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/lists/{listId}` Use this call to retrieve the following metadata for a specified list: | **Metadata** | **Description** | | --- | --- | | `category` | The category to which the list is assigned. | | `dataTags` | The data tags associated with the list. | | `hasSelectiveAccess` | If the value is true, this list uses [selective access](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Users/Selective_Access.html). | | `id` | The list ID. This corresponds to the `listId` parameter. | | `itemCount` | The number of list items present in the list. | | `managedBy` | Not currently used. | | `name` | The list name. | | `nextitemIndex` | Contains the index of the next new item in the list. Used only by numbered lists. | | `numberedList` | A true value indicates this is a numbered list. | | `parent` |

The parent hierarchy, which includes this information for the parent list:

* `id`
* `name`

| | `permittedItems` | Indicates how many more items you can add to the existing list. This value changes when the number of items in the list change. | | `productionData` | A true value indicates this is a production list in [Application Lifecycle Management (ALM)](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/ALM/Application_Lifecycle_Management.htm). | | `properties` |

The list properties, which include:

* `dataTags`
* `format`
* `formula`
* `notes`
* `referencedBy`

| | `subsets` |

A list of subsets associated with the list hierarchy, which includes the following information for the parent list:

* `id`
* `name`

| | `topLevelItem` | If present, indicates the top level item in a hierarchy. | | `useTopLevelAsPageDefault` | A true value indicates this list is the default item in a page selector. | | `workflowEnabled` | A true value indicates this list is enabled for [Workflow](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Modeling/Manage_Models/Workflow.html). | If any of the metadata values are not set for a list (for example, no parent or no top level item), then the result omits the metadata entry. **Notes**: * To use this call, you must be a [Workspace Administrator](https://help.anaplan.com/anapedia/Content/Administration_and_Security/Workspace_Administration.html). * Use of this call may be rate limited. If you call too frequently, you may receive a `429` response. ### Request ``` curl -X GET 'https://api.anaplan.com/2/0/workspaces/{workspaceId}/models/{modelId}/lists/{listId}' \ -H 'Accept: application/json' \ -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' ``` ### Request headers | **Header** | **Details** | | --- | --- | | `Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken {anaplan_auth_token}` |

* Example: `c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6`

bulkDeleteModelsFailures.description: Models that could not be deleted, with failure reasons. Omitted from the response when all requested deletions succeed.

id.description: 12-digit numeric identifier. The prefix 102 marks this as a module.
12- or 13-digit numeric identifier. Default views share the module prefix 102; named saved views use higher-range prefixes (up to 13 digits).

periodText.description: Human-readable label for the current period. Empty string when no current period is set.

id.description: 12-digit numeric identifier. The prefix 118 marks this as a process.

actionType.description: Category of action. Observed values include DELETE_BY_SELECTION. Distinguishes deletion actions from imports, exports, and processes.

calendarType.description: Calendar model used in this model. Determines the period hierarchy and week-based options.

extraWeekMonth.description: For 4-4-5 calendars, the month index (1–12) that receives the extra week in a 53-week year.

id.description: 12-digit numeric identifier. The prefix 101 marks this as a list.

listId.description: Anaplan ID of the parent hierarchy list this item belongs to. Only present when the list has a Parent Hierarchy configured.

listName.description: Name of the parent hierarchy list this item belongs to. Only present when the list has a Parent Hierarchy configured.

subsets.description: Subset membership flags keyed by subset name. true means the item belongs to that subset. Only present when includeAll=true.

properties.description: Custom list property values keyed by property name. Values are string-encoded regardless of the underlying property type. Only present when includeAll=true.

ListReadRequest.description: Async read-request token returned by POST .../lists/{listId}/readRequests. Poll the returned url to retrieve results when requestState is COMPLETE.

requestState.description: Current state of the async read operation.

url.description: URL to poll for the read-request result or download pages.

Version.description: The version of data being used in an Anaplan model

isActual.description: Whether this version holds actual (historical) data rather than forecast data.

isCurrent.description: Whether this is the currently active version, i.e. the version whose data covers the current period.

Switchover.description: The date that the version switches over from actual to forecast

ViewData.description: JSON-formatted view cell data returned when Accept: application/json and format=v1 are set on GET /models/{modelId}/views/{viewId}/data.

pages.description: Selected item labels for each page-axis dimension; empty when no page dimensions exist.

columnCoordinates.description: Coordinate labels for each column; each inner array has one entry per column dimension.

ViewDataRow.description: One row of view cell data.

rowCoordinates.description: Coordinate labels for this row; one entry per row dimension.

cells.description: Cell values in column order; positions correspond to columnCoordinates.

ViewReadRequest.description: Result record for a large-view read operation. Tracks the asynchronous read state and provides a URL for downloading the data once it is ready.

Chunk.description: A unit of partial file content

exportType.description: Shape and scope of the exported data.

id.description: 12-digit numeric identifier. The prefix 116 marks this as an export definition; the export output file shares this same ID.

File.description: A file associated with an import data source, import failure dump, or export

encoding.description: Character encoding of the file. Observed values: ISO-8859-1, UTF-16LE, UTF-8.

firstDataRow.description: Row number of the first data row. 0 means no data rows are configured; otherwise 1-based (row 1 = first row of the file).

headerRow.description: 1-based row number of the column-header row in a delimited file.

id.description: 12-digit numeric identifier. Import source files use the prefix 113; export output files use 116, matching their parent export's ID.

origin.description: Source type of the file: 'NATIVE_ANAPLAN' for Anaplan-owned files, 'FILE_BASED' for externally uploaded import sources.

id.description: 12-digit numeric identifier. The prefix 112 marks this as an import definition.

importType.description: Category of data the import processes.

name.description: Display name of the import definition

type.description: Data source type for this import: 'FILE' for file-based imports, 'MODEL' for model-to-model imports.

source.description: Source file configuration for file-based imports

failures.description: An array of cell write failures; empty when all cells updated successfully.

requestIndex.description: Index of the cell write request from the request array.

Task.description: A task tracking the execution of a process/import/export/action

creationTime.description: Task creation time in milliseconds since epoch

currentStep.description: Human-readable description of the current execution step

progress.description: Execution progress as a fraction (0.0–1.0); typically 1.0 on completion

result.description: Task result object, populated after the task reaches a terminal state (COMPLETE or CANCELLED). Absent while the task is NOT_STARTED or IN_PROGRESS.

TaskResult.description: The result of a completed task

failureDumpAvailable.description: Whether a failure dump file was generated for this task. When true, the dump can be downloaded via the failure dump endpoint for this object type.

objectId.description: Numeric ID of the Anaplan object (import, export, process, or action) that this task executed against.

objectName.description: Display name of the Anaplan object that this task executed against.

TaskWithUrl.description: Initial task reference returned by POST .../tasks. Contains the taskId and a URL for polling status.

creationTime.description: Task creation time in milliseconds since epoch.

taskId.description: Unique identifier for the task; use this to poll GET .../tasks/{taskId}.

taskUrl.description: URL for polling task status via GET.

Status.description: Response status object present in every Anaplan API response envelope. `code` is the Anaplan-specific status code (not the HTTP status code); `message` is the human-readable outcome.

code.description: Anaplan API status code for this response. Distinct from the HTTP status code.

message.description: Human-readable description of the status outcome.

Meta.description: Response metadata envelope present in paginated Anaplan API responses. `schema` is a URL to the JSON schema definition for the response body; `paging` holds cursor-based navigation state.

schema.description: URL to the JSON schema definition for this response body. A URL string, not an inline JSON Schema object.
